title: Mandatory Publishing, Returns, Alternate Exchanges, and Route Audits · Mastery Check
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript messaging decisions for mandatory publishing, returns, alternate exchanges, and route audits and requires the smallest defensible unroutable publication detection and ownership record with changed-case evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for unroutable publication detection and ownership record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defen…
objectives: Register return handling before mandatory publication and correlate returned content, exchange, routing key, properties, and publisher intent.
Distinguish broker confirms from consumer processing and combine mandatory returns or alternate routing with confirm results.
Build topology fixtures that assert expected destination sets, forbidden routes, unroutable cases, permissions, and changed bindings.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Alternate Return Tradeoff
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz messaging team handles case 7848 in a privacy-sensitive audit fanout with an offline subscriber. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to keep mandatory return, alternate custody, confirmation, and route uncertainty separate; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then fail the canary and prove older consumers remain compatible during rollback. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose mandatory returns, alternate exchanges, or both from publisher feedback, broker custody, operational ownership, and observability needs.
